title: Sentinel Shield production release authorization record
description: A version-controlled, two-person, time-boxed record that AUTHORIZES publishing a specific release CANDIDATE (identified by its reproducible manifest hash) at a given stage/scope. Consumed by scripts/authorize-production-release.sh. The authorization is NOT a publish action: the tool never creates a tag or GitHub Release; it emits a decision and prints the exact operator commands. Publishing still requires an explicit destructive flag AND this authorization token per repo policy. The record must match the candidate's version, stage, release_scope, source_commit, tag and candidate_hash, be unexpired, and be approved by a DIFFERENT person than the requester. Contains NO secrets, tokens as plaintext credentials, or key paths — the authorization.token is an opaque, non-credential correlation handle (e.g. a signed-commit id or an interactive-confirmation nonce).

schema_version.description: Schema version. Only "1" is supported; consumers fail closed on anything else.

version.description: Release version label being authorized, e.g. '2.0.0'. Must equal the candidate version.

stage.description: Release stage being authorized. Production authorization applies to beta|rc|ga (alpha is never a production release). Must equal the candidate stage.

release_scope.description: Governance track being authorized. Must equal the candidate release_scope. NOTE: only 'engine-only' can be authorized to GA by this tool; 'framework-validated'/'full-platform' GA remains BLOCKED because the engine cannot prove framework live-validation.

source_commit.description: The exact default-branch source commit whose CI proves the candidate. Must equal the candidate source_commit and the evidence engine_commit.

tag.description: The tag name that will be published for this release, e.g. 'v2.0.0'. Must equal the candidate tag.

candidate_hash.description: The reproducibility.hash of the release manifest (schemas/release-manifest.schema.json) that this record authorizes. Binds the authorization to an exact, reproducible release fingerprint: if the candidate content changes, its hash changes and this record no longer applies (fail closed).

authorization.description: Who authorized the release and how. method 'signed' means an out-of-band signed approval (token is the signed object/commit id); method 'interactive' means a human confirmed at a TTY (token is the confirmation nonce that must be re-supplied via --confirm-token). Either way requested_by != approved_by.

token.description: Opaque, NON-credential correlation handle for the authorization (signed object id or interactive nonce). Never a password, API token, or key path.

approved_by.description: GitHub login of the approver. MUST differ from requested_by (self-approval is forbidden); enforced by authorize-production-release.sh.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ) the authorization was created.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). The authorization only applies while expires_at is in the future; an expired record is rejected (fail closed).

$comment: Cross-field rules enforced by scripts/authorize-production-release.sh and not expressible in plain JSON Schema: version/stage/release_scope/source_commit/tag/candidate_hash must equal the verified candidate; authorization.requested_by != authorization.approved_by; expires_at must be in the future; interactive method requires --confirm-token to equal authorization.token.
